title: Female Pronouns and Verbs in Daily Dialogue
summary: This session focuses on using female pronouns and verbs in context, practicing dialogues with vocabulary related to daily activities. Students learn new terms like 'majalla' (magazine), 'maqtaba' (library), and review correct verb forms for females such as 'khrajti', 'hafizti'. The teacher emphasizes the structure of questions using 'anna' and 'adhunnu', distinguishing between past and present tense verbs. Students drill conversations about reading habits, routines, and familial roles.
key_points: Female pronouns and verb conjugations: e.g., خرجت (khrajti) for 'I exited', حفظت (hafizat) for 'I memorized'.
Using 'anna' and 'aadhnuthu':分辨现在和过去式。例如：अधन्नु अन (adhunnu anna) 表示 '我认为...'，而आदनवथउ (aadhnathu) 表示 '我曾经认为...'。
Dialogue practice on daily routines: 如何询问和回答关于阅读的对话，例如 'ماَتَ قْرأتِ؟' 和 'قرأت مَجَلَةٌ مِن Paki.'
Vocabulary related to religious studies and family roles: سورah memorization, siblings (أخوك), familial terms like 'mother' and 'daughter'.
questions: [01:02.18] Q: Why is zameenatu spelled with a fatha? — A: Because it's feminine, as classmates here are female.
[03:04.86] Q: When to use 'hafizti' vs 'hafazta'? — A: Use 'hafizti' for females in present tense; context determines person.
